channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: China at Ruskya nag-ugat ng militar drill sa mga ekonomikong agwat ng Japan
item.description: Ang mga bansang Tsina at Rusya ay nag-ugat ng militar drill sa mga ekonomikong agwat ng Japan, isang kritikal na teritoryo na nagmumula sa mga pananalapi ng bansa. Ang Tokyo ay nagsabi na ang aktibida...

item.title: USCIS Provides Clear Steps for Green Card Holders to Apply for U.S. Citizenship
item.description: The U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) has outlined a clear process for lawful permanent residents seeking naturalisation. Applicants must meet eligibility requirements, create an onlin...

item.title: Community Support Efforts Aid Ontarian Wildfire Evacuees
item.description: As wildfires continue burning across northwestern Ontario, over 1,800 evacuees have been relocated to cities like Thunder Bay and Toronto. Organizers report being overwhelmed by donations of clothing,...

item.title: Nuclear Powers Compete to Build Reactors in Africa for Energy and Industrialization
item.description: Africa is rapidly entering the nuclear age as the United States, Russia, and China vie to shape the continent's energy future. The International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) predicts that nuclear capac...

item.title: India Condemns Russia Over Attack on Vessel Killing Four Seafarers Near Ukraine Coast
item.description: India has summoned Russia's Charge d’Affaires following an attack on a vessel off the Ukraine coast that resulted in the deaths of four Indian seafarers. The incident occurred on Sunday as the MV Gold...

item.title: South Africa's Water Crisis: Challenges and Government Response
item.description: The article highlights the ongoing water crisis in South Africa, where millions lack access to safe and reliable drinking water. Despite being a constitutional right, many communities, both rural and ...

item.title: PSX rises on oil price decline and US-Iran talks
item.description: The Pakistan Stock Exchange (PSX) benchmark index surged 2,000 points during intraday trading on Tuesday, driven by a decline in global oil prices and renewed US-Iran mediation efforts. The KSE-100 in...

item.title: Market volatility forces consumers to leave stores amid economic uncertainty
item.description: The article discusses a recent surge in market activity, where economic fluctuations have led to a surge in consumer behavior. However, this has created challenges for businesses as people are increas...

item.title: CCM reaffirms commitment to Zanzibar reconciliation agreement
item.description: Tanzania's ruling party CCM has reaffirmed its dedication to a political reconciliation agreement with opposition party ACT Wazalendo in Zanzibar, rejecting criticisms against the landmark accord sign...

item.title: FG integrates Digital ID into healthcare to boost Universal Health Coverage by 2030
item.description: The Federal Government is rolling out a plan to link digital IDs with Nigeria's healthcare system to speed up Universal Health Coverage (UHC) and improve patient care. This move aims to make health in...

item.title: Pauline Njoroge Leaves Jubilee Party to Join Linda Mwananchi Movement
item.description: Pauline Njoroge, a senior political communications strategist, has officially resigned from the Jubilee Party after 14 years of membership. In a statement, she announced her decision to fully commit t...

item.title: Nicaragua's Ortega Announces End to Elections, Consolidating Power
item.description: Nicaragua's President Daniel Ortega has declared that the country will no longer hold elections, effectively eliminating any potential path for the opposition to challenge his government. This announc...

item.title: Trump's delayed response to soldier deaths sparks criticism over lack of empathy
item.description: President Donald Trump's delayed and dismissive reaction to the deaths of two American soldiers killed in Iranian drone attacks has drawn sharp criticism. The soldiers were killed in Jordan and northe...

item.title: Search continues for two missing boys at Nutimik Lake
item.description: Winnipeg search teams are persistently looking for two boys who went missing from Nutimik Lake, emphasizing the importance of maintaining hope despite challenging conditions. The article highlights th...

item.title: Gauteng taxi industry and transport MEC clash over licensing and leadership disputes
item.description: The Gauteng taxi industry has warned of a potential protest march within seven days if unresolved grievances against transport MEC Kedibone Diale-Tlabela are not addressed. The Gauteng National Taxi A...

item.title: Grassroots Educare Trust empowers children through early childhood development
item.description: Grassroots Educare Trust, founded in 1972, has been dedicated to improving early childhood development (ECD) in vulnerable communities across South Africa for over five decades. Based in Plettenberg B...

item.title: Akeredolu's Legacy: Trust and Leadership in Nigerian Politics
item.description: The article commemorates the 70th birthday of Arakunrin Oluwarotimi Odunayo Akeredolu, former Ondo State Governor, highlighting his leadership style, trust in aides, and commitment to governance. Aker...

item.title: Texas Roadhouse celebrates 10 years in the Philippines and plans regional expansion
item.description: Texas Roadhouse, a popular American steakhouse chain, is celebrating its 10th anniversary in the Philippines. The restaurant has been operating in the country since 2016 and has become a staple in Man...

item.title: Millie Mackintosh reflects on moving out of her former marital home and embracing a new chapter
item.description: Millie Mackintosh has shared insights into her decision to move out of the marital home she once shared with her former partner, Hugo Taylor. Describing the transition as a 'healing' process, the cele...

item.title: Niger Delta Economic Renaissance: From Oil Dependence to Blue Economy Opportunities
item.description: In a detailed address, Timi outlined a comprehensive strategy to transform the Niger Delta from an oil-dependent region into a diversified economic hub. He emphasized the need for institutionalizing b...
